title: Cognitive Warfare — counter-intelligence doctrine, operationalized into analytical instruments

fields.vision: A combined counter-intelligence and cognitive-warfare doctrine page.
fields.mechanism: Each of the three doctrinal authorities is paired with its constructive operationalization — not "what does Olson say" but "what analytical move does Olson's principle enable and what failure mode does it catch." Olson Olson principles, constructively operationalized Each Olson CI principle paired with the analytical move it enables and the failure mode it catches. The operationalization is what turns the principles into instruments.
fields.built_components: The page renders with the doctrinal operationalization in place across all three authorities. The construction workspace is in place; new CI instruments can be authored against the operationalized doctrine. Open work: source-register binding to specific passages in Olson, Wettering, and Sims is the next phase.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: Wire the doctrinal-operationalization layer through the source-register pipeline so each operationalized principle traces to a specific admitted passage. Once that binding lands, the doctrine page becomes the place where doctrinal claims are checkable, not just asserted. Step 01 Source-register binding Doctrinal-operationalization layer threaded through the source-register pipeline. Step 02 Principle-to-passage trace Each operationalized principle traceable to a specific admitted passage in the doctrinal corpus. Step 03 Checkable-doctrine page Doctrine page becomes the place where doctrinal claims are checkable, not just asserted.
fields.status: Status: The page renders with the doctrinal operationalization in place across all three authorities. The construction workspace is in place; new CI instruments can be authored against the operationalized doctrine. Open work: source-register binding to specific passages in Olson, Wettering, and Sims is the next phase.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: wire the doctrinal-operationalization layer through the source-register pipeline so each operationalized principle traces to a specific admitted passage. Once that binding lands, the doctrine page becomes the place where doctrinal claims are checkable, not just asserted.
fields.detailed_source_vision: A combined counter-intelligence and cognitive-warfare doctrine page. Operationalizes the substantive doctrinal corpus — Olson, Wettering, Sims — into actual analytical instruments rather than treating it as a reading list. The premise is that CI doctrine works as a constructive discipline: each doctrinal principle is paired with the analytical move it enables and the failure mode it guards against. The page is research-relevant in the strong sense — the place where doctrinal CI literature is operationalized rather than reviewed.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: Each of the three doctrinal authorities is paired with its constructive operationalization — not "what does Olson say" but "what analytical move does Olson's principle enable and what failure mode does it catch." Olson Olson principles, constructively operationalized Each Olson CI principle paired with the analytical move it enables and the failure mode it catches. The operationalization is what turns the principles into instruments.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.
